data.title: Network gear maker Ubiquiti beats Q4 revenue, adjusted EPS estimates; authorizes stock buyback
data.description:
Overview

  • Ubiquiti Q4 fiscal 2025 revenue rises 49.6% yr/yr, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q4 fiscal 2025 beats estimates, reaching $3.54

  • Company announces $500 mln stock repurchase program

Outlook

  • Company plans regular quarterly dividends of at least $0.80 per share in FY 2026

  • Ubiquiti authorizes $500 mln stock repurchase program

Result Drivers

  • ENTERPRISE TECHNOLOGY - Revenue growth driven by Enterprise Technology platform, contributing significantly to overall increase

  • GROSS MARGIN IMPROVEMENT - Favorable product mix and reduced inventory charges led to higher gross margins

  • TAX BENEFIT - Deferred tax asset recognition contributed to increased net income

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

Q4 Revenue

Beat

$759.20 mln

$634.10 mln (2 Analysts)

Q4 Adjusted EPS

Beat

$3.54

$2.23 (1 Analyst)

Q4 EPS

$4.41

Q4 Adjusted Net Income

$214.40 mln

Q4 Gross Margin

45.1%

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy", 1 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the communications & networking peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Ubiquiti Inc is $343.50, about 13.7% below its August 21 closing price of $390.57

  • The stock recently traded at 47 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 39 three months ago

Press Release:

data.title: US Natgas Prices Remain Near 15-Month Low
data.description:
US natural gas futures fell to $2.8/MMBtu on Friday, reversing part of a 2.7% gain from the previous session and hovering near their lowest since November 2024, as supply remained abundant.

Production in the Lower 48 states averaged a record 108.1 bcfd in August, slightly above July’s 107.9 bcfd, though daily output recently dipped to 106.4 bcfd, a six-week low.

Despite hotter-than-usual summer temperatures, strong supply has allowed storage levels to remain well above average, about 5.8% higher than typical for this time of year.

However, weekly injections into storage were below the five-year norm, suggesting limited pressure from demand.

LNG export flows have risen modestly, averaging 15.8 bcfd in August. With weather expected near seasonal norms through early September, demand is unlikely to spike, keeping prices under pressure despite occasional short-term gains.

data.title: REG - PetroTal Corp. - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 4744W PetroTal Corp. 22 August 2025  

Transaction in Own Shares

Calgary, AB and Houston, TX - August 22, 2025 - PetroTal Corp. ("PetroTal" or the "Company") (TSX: TAL, AIM: PTAL and OTCQX: PTALF), announces that, in accordance with the terms of its share buyback programme announced on 30 July 2025, it has purchased the following number of common shares of no par value each in the capital of the Company ("Common Shares") through Stifel Nicolaus Europe Limited ("Stifel"). The repurchased shares will be cancelled.

Date of purchase:

21 August 2025

0.670 CAD

0.670 CAD

Following the repurchased shares, the Company will have 913,825,570 Common Shares of no par value each in issue and no Treasury Shares. Therefore, the total voting rights in the Company will be 913,825,570.

The figure of 913,825,570 may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.      

In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 (the Market Abuse Regulation) as in force in the UK by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, the table below contains detailed information of the individual trades made by Stifel as part of the buyback programme.

For further information, please contact:

ABOUT PETROTAL

PetroTal is a publicly traded, tri‐quoted (TSX: TAL, AIM: PTAL and OTCQX: PTALF) oil and gas development and production Company domiciled in Calgary, Alberta, focused on the development of oil assets in Peru.  PetroTal's flagship asset is its 100% working interest in Bretana oil field in Peru's Block 95 where oil production was initiated in June 2018.  In early 2022, PetroTal became the largest crude oil producer in Peru.  The Company's management team has significant experience in developing and exploring for oil in Peru and is led by a Board of Directors that is focused on safely and cost effectively developing the Bretana oil field. It is actively building new initiatives to champion community sensitive energy production, benefiting all stakeholders.

For further information, please see the Company's website at www.petrotal-corp.com, the Company's filed documents at www.sedar.com, or below:

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 7 AM ET: Global Markets Largely Flat Ahead of Powell's Speech | Powell ...
data.description:
Global Markets Largely Flat Ahead of Powell's Speech

Trading was mostly rangebound early Friday, with investors jittery ahead of the Federal Reserve chair's annual address at Jackson Hole.

----

Powell Plans U-Turn on an Economic Strategy That Soured

The Fed unveiled a strategy five years ago for worries that the economy outgrew. Now, it will formally reset.

----

Can Germany's Merz Revive the Economy? Voters Are Running Out of Patience

Polls show voters are growing restless with Friedrich Merz, raising pressure on the chancellor to deliver on the domestic stage.

----

Eurozone Wage Growth Gains Pace, Likely Adding to ECB Caution

Wages in the eurozone rose at a slightly faster pace during the three months through June as unemployment remained at record lows.

----

U.K. Consumers Sunnier as BOE Cuts Rates But Economic Fears Remain

U.K. consumers felt a little better about their finances this month as the Bank of England lowered borrowing costs, though sentiment remains weak amid wider economic turmoil.

----

How Trump Will Decide Which Chips Act Companies Must Give Up Equity

The administration isn't eyeing equity in semiconductor companies that are increasing U.S. investment like TSMC and Micron, but may take stakes in others.

----

Copper Is Struggling. European Defense Spending Could Help.

In the short term, copper may have farther to fall.

----

Peter Thiel Leads Pack of Investors Piling Into Ether

Proponents see Ethereum becoming the platform of choice for the financial world.

----

Japan's Inflation Stays Well Above BOJ's 2% Target

Japan's consumer inflation cooled in July, but remained well above the central bank's target of 2%, reinforcing expectations of a coming interest-rate increase.

----

Canada PM Carney Touts 'Productive' Call With President Trump Over Trade

Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney and President Trump agreed to reconvene shortly to discuss further.

----

Business Activity Gains Pace as Manufacturing Rebounds

U.S. business activity expanded at its fastest pace this year as the factory sector rebounded strongly, though trade tariffs were fueling inflation in input prices, according to the S&P Global Flash U.S. Composite PMI.

----

Home Sales Surprisingly Rose in July While Prices Eased

The unexpected pickup in sales activity reflected a slight improvement in affordability as home-price growth slows.

----

High Auto Tariffs on EU Will Stay Until Bloc Moves to Reduce Levies

The auto tariffs will decrease to 15% when the European Union introduces legislation to reduce rates on U.S. goods.

The Biggest Retailers Are Thriving in the Tariff Economy

Walmart, Amazon and T.J. Maxx are beating out rivals by offering deals to stressed-out consumers.

OpenAI to Open New Delhi Office as It Bulks Up India Presence

----

Kazatomprom Posts Lower Profit, Revenue But Backs Production Guidance

While long-term Uranium prices have remained stable despite the volatility in the market, the company doesn't plan to return to 100% production levels at this time.

----

Gold Fields Earnings Boosted by Higher Gold Price

Net profit was $1.02 billion for the first half of 2025 compared with $389 million for the same period last year.

----

Tesla Hasn't Filed Crash Reports on Time. Federal Investigators Want to Know Why.

Some accidents involving the EV's automated driving technology weren't submitted for months.

----

Air Canada Expects to Return Close to Full Schedule by Friday

The Montreal-based carrier said it expects to "deliver close to its full network schedule" by Friday. Its previous guidance indicated it would take seven to 10 days to return to full capacity.

----

Boeing in Talks to Sell China Hundreds of Planes as Part of U.S. Trade Deal

The purchase is envisioned as a component of a more expansive trade deal if the world's two largest economies can reach an agreement in the next few months.

----

FanDuel Places Opening Bet on Promising but Precarious Prediction Markets

The sports-betting platform's deal with the exchange company CME Group could lead to its making markets for sports-related events.

----

NBCUniversal and MLB Nearing 3-Year Deal Approaching $600 Million

Netflix is also in advanced talks with the league to stream the Home Run Derby in a deal that would be worth more than $105 million.

----

Fonterra to Sell Consumer Businesses to Lactalis

The New Zealand dairy co-operative has agreed to sell its consumer businesses to Lactalis in a deal valued at $2.23 billion.

----

Intuit Sales Jump 20%, Expects Slower Growth Ahead

The tax preparation software maker's sales rose by a fifth in its latest quarter, boosted by growth across each of its segments, and it expects double-digit revenue gains to continue.

data.title: Linn Andersson new member of Boliden's Group Management
data.description:
Boliden

Linn Andersson has been appointed EVP Strategy and CTO. The role, which is new to Boliden's Group Management, includes overall responsibility for strategy, business and technology development as well as procurement.

"Linn Andersson has solid experience of our operations combined with very good leadership skills. She will thereby add further valuable expertise to the Group Management and contribute to strengthening the conditions for Boliden's long-term value creation", says Mikael Staffas, President and CEO of Boliden.

Linn Andersson has an engineering degree in Chemistry from Umeå University and an MBA from Edinburgh Business School. During her career, she has primarily held several leading roles within Boliden Rönnskär, most recently as General Manager with overall responsibility for the business unit.

For further information, please contact:

Klas Nilsson

Director Group Communications

+46 70 453 65 88

Klas.Nilsson@boliden.com

Metals for generations to come

Boliden's vision is to be the most climate-friendly and respected metal provider in the world. We are Europe's producer of sustainable metals and, guided by our values care, courage and responsibility, we operate within exploration, mines, smelters and recycling. We are around 8,000 employees and have annual revenues of approximately SEK 90 billion. The share is listed in the Large Cap segment of NASDAQ Stockholm.

www.boliden.com

https://news.cision.com/boliden/r/linn-andersson-new-member-of-boliden-s-group-management%2Cc4221277

Press release

data.title: Ubiquiti Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Ubiquiti Inc., a leading provider of networking technology for service providers and enterprises, has released its Form 10-K report for fiscal year 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth, strategic initiatives, and the challenges the company faces in a dynamic market environment.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: Ubiquiti Inc. reported total revenues of $2,573.5 million, marking a 33.4% increase from fiscal 2024. This growth was driven by both the Enterprise Technology and Service Provider Technology platforms.

Gross Profit: The company achieved a gross profit of $1,117.5 million, with a gross profit margin of 43.4%, up from 38.4% in fiscal 2024. This improvement was primarily due to a favorable product mix and lower excess and obsolete inventory charges.

Income from Operations: Income from operations reached $836.3 million, accounting for 32% of total revenues, up from 26% in fiscal 2024. This reflects improved operational efficiency.

Net Income: Net income surged to $711.9 million, an increase of 103.4% from fiscal 2024. The effective tax rate decreased to 11.6% due to a one-time deferred tax benefit.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Ubiquiti categorizes its solutions into Enterprise Technology and Service Provider Technology. Enterprise Technology includes platforms like UniFi Cloud Gateways, UniFi WiFi, and UniFi Protect, while Service Provider Technology includes platforms such as airMAX, UISP, and airFiber.

Geographical Performance: North America saw a 36.9% increase in revenues, driven by Enterprise Technology products. EMEA experienced a 35.0% increase due to both Enterprise and Service Provider Technology products. Asia Pacific revenues increased by 32.0%, primarily from Enterprise Technology products, while South America saw a decrease of 3.7% in revenues.

Sales Units: The company sells its products globally through a network of over 100 distributors and direct sales through webstores, with a significant portion of sales occurring outside the United States.

New Product Launches: Ubiquiti continues to expand its product portfolio with offerings like UniFi Talk, a business phone system, and Wave Technology products for high-throughput wireless deployments.

Research and Development: The R&D team, consisting of 1,187 full-time equivalent employees, focuses on developing new products and versions of existing products. R&D expenses increased to $169.7 million, reflecting ongoing investment in innovation.

Manufacturing and Suppliers: The company relies on contract manufacturers in Vietnam and China and faces risks related to component shortages and supply chain disruptions. Efforts to mitigate these include increasing inventory and modifying product designs.

Future Outlook: Ubiquiti anticipates continued growth in its Enterprise Technology segment and plans to increase R&D personnel and expenses. It is also monitoring geopolitical tensions and their potential impact on operations.

Strategic Initiatives

Supply Chain Management: Ubiquiti has focused on managing supply constraints by increasing inventory build and modifying product designs to leverage alternate suppliers. The company is also monitoring geopolitical tensions and their potential impact on operations, particularly in regions like Ukraine and Taiwan.

Capital Management: The company repaid $175 million on its Revolving Facility and $283.1 million on its Term Loan Facilities during fiscal 2025. It also paid $145.2 million in dividends on common stock. Ubiquiti maintains a cash and cash equivalents balance of $149.7 million as of June 30, 2025, and has a strategy to ensure liquidity through existing cash, cash equivalents, and credit facilities.

Future Outlook: Ubiquiti intends to pay regular quarterly cash dividends of at least $0.80 per share during each remaining quarter of fiscal 2025, subject to Board approval. It plans to establish a new credit facility before the existing one matures in March 2026 to meet long-term liquidity requirements. The company is also monitoring the impact of the OECD's global minimum corporate tax, which may affect future tax rates and cash tax payments.

Challenges and Risks

Market and Operational Risks: Ubiquiti faces significant challenges in accurately forecasting future sales due to its reliance on distributors who do not provide long-term purchase commitments or reliable market demand information. This lack of visibility can lead to overproduction or underproduction, impacting inventory levels and financial performance. Additionally, the company is exposed to risks from rapid technological changes and intense competition in the markets it serves, which require continuous innovation and competitive pricing to maintain market position.

Supply Chain Disruptions: The company is vulnerable to supply chain disruptions, particularly due to its reliance on a limited number of contract manufacturers and suppliers. Geopolitical tensions, such as the military conflict between Russia and Ukraine and escalating tensions between China and Taiwan, pose risks to component availability and manufacturing capacity. Natural disasters and public health crises, like COVID-19, further exacerbate these risks by potentially disrupting manufacturing and logistics operations.

Cybersecurity Threats: Cybersecurity threats are a growing concern, with the company acknowledging past cyber-attacks and the potential for future breaches that could compromise sensitive data and disrupt operations. The increasing use of AI technologies introduces additional compliance and reputational risks, as these technologies may lead to unintended biases or errors.

Management Challenges: Management highlights the need to effectively manage product introductions and transitions to stimulate customer demand and maintain revenue growth. The company is investing in new growth areas, such as enterprise and service provider technologies, but acknowledges that returns on these investments may be lower or slower than expected. The company also faces challenges in managing its growth, particularly in maintaining efficient operations and control systems as it expands its product offerings and global presence.

Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates and interest rates, which could adversely affect its financial results. Inflation and global economic instability, driven by factors such as trade tensions and geopolitical conflicts, may impact consumer demand and increase operational costs. The company is also at risk from changes in global trade policies and tariffs, which could disrupt supply chains and increase the cost of goods sold.

SEC Filing: Ubiquiti Inc. [ UI ] - 10-K - Aug. 22, 2025

data.title: Tyra Biosciences Price Target Maintained With a $30.00/Share by HC Wainwright & Co.
These Stocks Are Moving the Most Today: Nvidia, Intuit, Zoom, Workday, Palantir, and More — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Joe Woelfel and George Glover

Stock futures were rising Friday, with the S&P 500 bidding to snap a five-session losing streak, as Wall Street awaited clues on interest rates from Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell in a speech scheduled at the central bank's annual gathering in Jackson Hole, Wyo.

These stocks were poised to make moves Friday:

Intuit topped Wall Street's fiscal fourth-quarter earnings expectations but the stock fell 5.6% in premarket trading following disappointing fiscal 2026 guidance. The parent company of TurboTax and Credit Karma said it expects fiscal-year earnings of between $22.98 and $23.18 a share on revenue of $21 billion to $21.19 billion. The low end of Intuit's guidance was just below Wall Street estimates for earnings of $23.02 a share on revenue of $21.1 billion.

Workday reported second-quarter adjusted earnings of $2.21 a share, beating analysts' estimates of $2.11, as subscription revenue jumped 14% from a year earlier to $2.17 billion. But the stock stumbled, falling 4.4%, as the human-resources software company issued third-quarter guidance that was slightly shy of expectations. For the current quarter ending in October, Workday expects subscription revenue of $2.235 billion, just below estimates of $2.237 billion. Workday also announced it was acquiring Paradox, a company that uses artificial intelligence to automate recruitment and hiring processes. Terms of the deal weren't disclosed.

Second-quarter sales at Ross Stores rose 5% to $5.53 billion, with the discount department store chain saying it was encouraged by trends at the end of the quarter, "particularly with the early sales performance related to the back-to-school selling season." Wall Street was estimating sales of $5.54 billion. Earnings of $1.56 a share included a negative impact of 11 cents from tariff-related costs, the retailer said. A year earlier, Ross Stores reported a profit of $1.59 a share. The stock climbed 2.2% in premarket trading.

Zoom Communications reported second-quarter adjusted earnings of $1.53 a share, beating analysts' forecasts of $1.38, as revenue rose 4.7% to a slightly better-than-expected $1.22 billion. The videoconferencing platform also raised its fiscal-year outlook, sending the stock up 5.2% in the premarket session.

Paramount Skydance was up 0.4% ahead of the opening bell after jumping 15% on Thursday. It wasn't immediately clear what drove the surge, although shares have drawn significant interest from retail investors in recent weeks. Paramount and Skydance merged on Aug. 7. to form a new company officially called Paramount, a Skydance Corporation. After floundering for its first couple of trading sessions, the stock spiked 37% on Aug. 13, in a move that appeared to echo the 2021 meme-stock craze.

Palantir Technologies declined 0.9% in premarket trading after the company, which sells AI software to manage and analyze large amounts of data, rose slightly Thursday to snap a six-session losing streak. It was Palantir's longest losing streak since April 2024, when it also declined for six consecutive trading days, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

Shares of Nvidia, the leading maker of AI chips, fell 1.2% after multiple reports said the company had asked some of its suppliers to stop production related to its H20 processors, which are made for the Chinese market. The stock has finished lower for three consecutive days, falling 3.9% over the span, and has closed down in four of the past five sessions.

Lucid Group shares dropped 1.9% after the electric-vehicle maker said it would proceed with a 1-for-10 reverse stock split of its common stock, following approval by its board. The reverse stock split is expected to take effect after next Friday's closing bell, and the stock is expected to start trading on a split-adjusted basis when the market opens on Sept. 2.

Earnings reports are expected Friday from BJ's Wholesale Club and RLX Technology.

Write to Joe Woelfel at joseph.woelfel@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: UK's FTSE 100 steady after record streak as markets await Powell's speech
data.description:
Key points:
  • FTSE 100 flat, FTSE 250 up 0.3%
  • UK consumers gain confidence after Bank of England rate cut, GfK says
  • StanChart shares rise after favourable ruling in U.S. litigation

Britain's FTSE 100 held steady on Friday, taking a breather after closing at record highs, as global investors turned their attention to Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's speech for insights into the future path of U.S. interest rates.

As of 1042 GMT, the blue-chip index CURRENCYCOM:UK100 was little changed and on pace to close the week up nearly 2%.

The FTSE 100 had scaled record closing levels in three consecutive sessions, helped by a market rotation away from technology stocks that triggered a selloff on Wall Street earlier this week.

Meanwhile, the midcap index FTSE 250 rose 0.3% and looked set to end the week with a modest 0.7% gain.

Global markets' attention now centres on the Jackson Hole symposium, where investors hope Powell will signal a September rate cut following recent U.S. labor market weakness.

Banking stocks in London (.FTNMX301010) led FTSE 100 gains, rising 0.5%, with Standard Chartered jumping 3% following a favourable U.S. Department of Justice ruling in a long-standing civil case.

The bank's shares tumbled last week when a U.S. Republican lawmaker called for a probe, claiming that it was involved in sanctions evasion.

The energy sector was up 0.3% as oil prices stabilized amid fading hopes for a Russia-Ukraine peace agreement.

A survey on Friday showed British consumers have turned a bit more confident this month after the latest interest rate cut by the Bank of England but are vulnerable to worries about rising inflation and potential tax increases.

Retailer names like Tesco , J Sainsbury and Unilever marginally dropped. The consumer stocks index also fell 0.3%. Despite this daily setback, consumer shares have demonstrated strength this week, rising nearly 3%.

data.title: China Tightens Controls on Rare Earths
data.description:
China, the world's largest rare-earth supplier, published new rules on Friday to strengthen oversight of rare-earth production and trading, further tightening its grip on supply of the critical minerals.

The regulations, published on the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology's website, set output controls on rare-earth mining and smelting, and requires companies to closely monitor and report product flows. The new rules took effect July 28.

Producers must record and submit monthly reports on the circulation of rare-earth products--critical for electric vehicles, jet fighters and wind turbines--to a government-run tracking system, according to the rules.

Under the rules, Beijing will assign production quotas to rare-earth mining and smelting companies, which are required to submit their output data and other relevant information to local authorities.

Those who fail to comply with the quota-based production and other requirements under the new rules may be subjected to administrative penalties, and their production quotas for the following year will be reduced accordingly. The ministry didn't specify the volume of the production quotas.

Friday's move came after the government imposed stricter export licensing requirements on rare-earth metals and related products in April. The restrictions led to a sharp drop in exports, including to the U.S. Outbound shipments only improved after Washington and Beijing reached a temporary trade deal.

As part of the trade truce, Beijing promised to resume rare-earth shipments to the U.S., while Washington agreed to remove restrictions on tech exports to China.

In July, China stepped up its crackdown on illegal rare-earth exports, citing national-security concerns.

Write to Singapore Editors at singaporeeditors@dowjones.com
